library_meta.name: IMS PAILs/RAILs Library
library_meta.frs_reference: Dr. Andreo Spina - Functional Anatomy Seminars - Aug 2024 flowchart

protocol_definitions.PAILs: Progressive Angular Isometric Loading - client contracts the stretched tissue by pushing INTO the end range
protocol_definitions.RAILs: Regressive Angular Isometric Loading - client contracts the antagonist to drive DEEPER into the end range
protocol_definitions.combined_use: PAILs + RAILs performed sequentially in the same end-range position = the full protocol

1.desc: Introduction to the PAILs/RAILs protocol. Focus on contracting the right tissue, learning to ramp up and down, understanding the position. Effort is intentionally sub-maximal so the client can own the mechanics.
cues: Find the stretch first, don't rush
Ramp up slow - 3 seconds to max effort at 30%
Focus on breathing, not grinding

2.desc: Client owns the mechanics. Now we push the intensity to drive actual capsular adaptation and neurological gains. This is where most clients live.
cues: Breathe through the contraction - don't hold
Ramp up to about 60% and hold it steady
Back off before you lose the position

3.desc: Reserved for experienced clients with good mechanics. Maximum ramp-up intent. This is where real tissue change happens - but only if the position is owned and breathing is preserved.
cues: Breathe - do not hold your breath through max effort
Slowest possible ramp up - this is where adaptation lives
Full body stays engaged through the contraction

indicated_when: Insufficient PROM with aberrant joint function (CAJP) on table test
Insufficient PROM with normal joint function (CAJR) + positive PAILs test
FP interpretation: mechanical tissue tension (PAILs after FR release)
contraindicated_when: Pain on passive test for that joint
Spasm finding - use Isometric Ramping Strategies instead
No improvement after 4 weeks -> Expanded Bioflow assessment + refer
programming_logic.selection_rule: Target the capsular direction(s) flagged as restricted in the table test. One joint may have multiple targets programmed.
programming_logic.session_integration: PAILs/RAILs block sits AFTER CARs and BEFORE strength work. Prescribed for flagged joints only - not all joints.
programming_logic.session_dose: 1-2 capsular targets per flagged joint per session. 2-3 rounds per target.
programming_logic.weekly_frequency: 2-3 sessions per week per target (not every session - recovery matters)
programming_logic.re_test_cadence: Re-test PROM at week 4. If improvement, progress level. If no change, consider FP interpretation deeper or refer.

level_progression.block_1: L1 for all new targets - learn the mechanics first
level_progression.block_2: L2 once client owns the ramp
level_progression.block_3_plus: L3 for adaptation blocks - only if position is owned

exercises.name: Lateral Toes Extension PAILs/RAILs - L3 (Maximal)
exercises.pail_instruction: Contract the stretched tissue - push INTO the end-range position at target effort level
exercises.rail_instruction: Contract the antagonist - pull deeper INTO the stretch, actively driving end range
cues: Breathe - do not hold your breath through max effort
Slowest possible ramp up - this is where adaptation lives
Full body stays engaged through the contraction
exercises.notes: Reserved for experienced clients with good mechanics. Maximum ramp-up intent. This is where real tissue change happens - but only if the position is owned and breathing is preserved.
